meaning: n.圆形之隆起物,肉峰,预感,大块vt.弯腰驼背,弓起背部,耸肩vi.向前移动,隆起
examples: I had a hunch (that) you'd be back.
It seemed that the doctor's hunch had been right.
Every hunch led to a blind alley and every hypothesis to a dead end.
Hunch will keep its current management team and continue to be based in New York.
Massa said he had a "hunch", but added it will not affect their relationship.
